gpt55_review_summary: GPT-5.5 reviewed the benchmark-runner ownership inversion, legacy wrapper export surface, _openai_compatible_engine compatibility, CLI help under both module paths, import-cycle behavior around document_kv_cache.openai_compatible, and the legacy monkeypatch bridge into document-owned main(). It found no issues and verified python -m pytest -q passed.
scope: document and legacy package README migration notes
verification: poetry run pytest tests/test_benchmark_runner.py tests/test_public_package.py tests/test_openai_compatible.py tests/test_live_server.py -q

poetry run pytest tests/test_benchmark_runner.py tests/test_public_package.py tests/test_benchmark_plan.py tests/test_benchmark_plan_executor.py tests/test_openai_compatible.py tests/test_live_server.py tests/test_vllm_smoke.py tests/test_release_evidence.py tests/test_project_governance.py::test_legacy_restaurant_imports_in_tests_are_explicitly_scoped -q
find src tests -name '*.py' -print0 | xargs -0 python -m py_compile
what_changed: Moved the V1 benchmark-runner implementation into document_kv_cache.benchmark_runner.
Turned restaurant_kv_serving.benchmark_runner into a compatibility wrapper over the document benchmark-runner module.
Kept document_kv_cache.benchmark_runner.__all__ curated while preserving the legacy wrapper's broader historical import surface and _openai_compatible_engine access.
Updated package READMEs and public-package tests to record the new implementation boundary.
why: The generalized package should own baseline-vs-cache benchmark execution directly because it is part of the public V1 release workflow for arbitrary documents. This is a behavior-preserving migration step toward retiring restaurant-specific implementation ownership.
